title: Protocol Synopsis - NSCLC First-line Phase 3
text:
Study Title: A randomized phase 3 trial in first-line metastatic NSCLC comparing investigational therapy plus SOC versus SOC alone.

Objectives: Primary objective is progression-free survival. Secondary objectives include overall survival, safety, and quality of life.

Population: Adults aged 18 years and older with histologically confirmed metastatic NSCLC, ECOG 0-1, and adequate organ function.

Key Exclusion: Active CNS metastases, severe infection, prior PD-1 inhibitor in metastatic setting.

Design: Approximately 620 participants, 1:1 randomization, global multicenter, treatment every 3 weeks.

Assessments: CT scans every 8 weeks, blood tests at each cycle, adverse event monitoring throughout.

Risks and Benefits: Potential treatment-related side effects include fatigue, rash, and liver enzyme elevation. Potential benefit is improved disease control.

Participant Rights: Participation is voluntary; participants may withdraw at any time without affecting routine care.

title: Protocol Synopsis - Rheumatoid Arthritis Phase 2
text:
Study Title: Phase 2 trial evaluating efficacy and safety of an investigational biologic in moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis.

Objectives: Evaluate reduction in disease activity scores at week 12 and characterize safety profile.

Population: Adults 18-70 years with confirmed rheumatoid arthritis and elevated inflammatory markers.

Key Exclusion: Active or recent severe infection, active tuberculosis, pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Design: 240 participants randomized 2:1 to investigational biologic or placebo plus standard background therapy.

Assessments: Joint assessment, patient-reported outcomes, laboratory safety tests, and adverse event checks.

Risks and Benefits: Possible infections, injection-site reactions, and laboratory abnormalities; possible improvement in symptoms.

Participant Rights: Informed consent required, optional participation, and withdrawal allowed at any time.
